title: Agent Audit — Argo vs Hermes vs Goose (+ Claude Code)
status: complete — 4-way deep-dive: code, docs, issues, local-Hermes delta, ND-vision filter

vision.statement: Argo's sharpened product vision: a trusted personal AI operator FOR neurodivergent people — built to support executive function and other ND traits (broad ND: dyslexia, dyscalculia, autism, ADHD, aphantasia, and more — not just autism+ADHD). This sharpens the manifesto's 'trusted digital operator' with a who and a why. Grounded in Argo's existing neurodivergent-first brain identity and the operator's own profile. The kernel-safety thesis (safe by construction) is itself an ND feature: it removes the vigilance burden. KEY FINDING: Hermes treats accessibility/ND as edge cases (voice-first, ADHD standups, proactive reminders exist but are not the spine); Goose has zero ND framing. Argo's wedge is to make ND executive-function support THE spine, not a side effect.

filters.name: Initiation friction
filters.desc: Reduces task activation cost — lowers the 'getting started' barrier (the core ADHD/exec-function wall).

filters.name: Externalized memory
filters.desc: Offloads working memory — remembers goals, context, where you left off, what was decided.

filters.name: Calm output
filters.desc: Low-arousal, low-firehose, sensory-regulating output — never a wall of noise.

filters.name: Low decision load
filters.desc: Sensible defaults; doesn't demand constant micro-choices (decision fatigue / analysis paralysis).

filters.name: Structure & follow-through
filters.desc: Breaks down, sequences, nudges, tracks — supports planning and follow-through deficits.

filters.name: Reliability / trust
filters.desc: Predictable, safe, honest — removes the vigilance burden. Safe-by-construction = ND feature.

vision.curationBias: RUTHLESS by design. Six escalating 'add to goal' messages is itself scope creep — so this audit's job is a SMALL curated pull-set and a LOT of explicit DROP. A feature being slick in a dev-tool (Goose) or broad in Hermes does not make it fit an ND executive-function assistant. Default verdict is DROP unless it scores ≥2 ND filters with low cognitive cost. Result: ~8 worthy pulls, ~15 explicit drops. Goose: ~80% of distinct features correctly DROP (dev-throughput agent). Hermes: breadth is the anti-pattern; the few ND signals it treats as edge cases are Argo's spine.

agents.name: Argo
agents.tagline: Trusted operator: knows the goal, enforces scope on every action, reports only verified output.

agents.name: Hermes (upstream)
agents.lang: Python (~25k tests, 70+ tools, 28 toolsets, 20 platform adapters)
agents.tagline: The self-improving AI agent — built-in learning loop, skills from experience, $5 VPS, text it from Telegram.
agents.vsArgo: Argo's parent. Argo hardens Hermes's breadth into a kernel-enforced safety contract + ND spine. Hermes maximizes capability for builders; Argo narrows to ND executive-function.

agents.name: Hermes (local customized)
agents.tagline: Jason's customized Hermes — the bar Argo set out to match then exceed.
agents.vsArgo: Customizations: gateway streaming dispatch (now upstreamed), ai-gateway provider plugin, browser_providers, telegram/cron tweaks.

agents.name: Goose
agents.tagline: Native open-source AI agent — desktop, CLI, API — for code & workflows.
agents.vsArgo: Different lineage (Block). Dev-team-throughput agent. ~80% of distinct features DROP for an ND assistant; two ideas worth pulling (top-of-mind reminders, interactive plan mode).

agents.name: Claude Code (reference)
agents.tagline: The reference harness — surveyed for plumbing/permission/runnability patterns.
agents.vsArgo: Study-only; reimplement clean. Findings are infra-level (permission protocol, per-model capability matrix, activity-feed extraction).

argoInventory.note: Argo's current surface — the dedupe baseline. 675 tests green (648 TS + 27 Rust).

categories.name: Architecture
items: Rust safety kernel (assess() gates every tool call: Allow/Ask/Block) — deterministic, FAIL-CLOSED
Approvals queue + goal ledger + event log
TS agent layer (Node22/ESM/tsx, no build step)
Kernel is the boundary — TS cannot bypass it

categories.name: TUI / UX
items: React/Ink streaming transcript + tool activity + spinner
Inline approvals, slash commands, custom composer
TUI-SMOOTH: clean per-tool activity feed (no JSON/temp-path firehose)

categories.name: Prompt
items: Skill index injected; recall loads full body

categories.name: Self-improvement
items: Background review → writes skills tagged argo-learned
Safe curator (archives only stale learned skills, never deletes)

categories.name: Autonomy / reach
items: MCP: consume (mount servers) + serve (Argo as MCP server)
Cron schedule, project rooms, operator modes, model routing

ndVisionAlignment.note: How Argo's EXISTING features already serve the ND vision — and where the gaps are.

strong.why: Removes vigilance burden — the operator never polices the agent; the boundary is physical. Stronger than Goose's fail-open LLM reviewer or Hermes's in-process approval.
Externalizes goals and context across sessions — offloads working memory; supports follow-through.
Builds a persistent model of the user; the ND framing is already encoded.
Just shipped — kills the firehose; low-arousal output. Argo LEADS all three on calm output.

gaps.gap: No explicit task-initiation / 'just start the next tiny step' affordance
gaps.note: PULL #1 — the activation wall is the central ND exec-function need; NONE of the 3 agents target it directly. Argo's differentiator.
PULL — Hermes 'converse mode' + Goose 'plan mode' both exist; Argo's autonomy L1 is adjacent but not an interactive front-door.

gaps.gap: No top-of-mind / pinned-reminder injected every turn
No proactive nudge cadence (gentle check-ins / standups)
gaps.note: PULL — Hermes users built ADHD standups + unprompted reminders on top; make it native.
PULL — Hermes shadow-git checkpoints; reduces anxiety around destructive ops.
Candidate — broad ND, not just exec function.

comparison.note: Decision-level matrix. Cells: ✓ has · ~ partial · ✗ lacks. Verdict from the ND filter. Refined by code+docs+issues deep-dive.

rows.note: Argo's structural moat — safe by construction. Goose adversary-mode fails OPEN (reviewer error → call allowed).
Core ND differentiator; reinforced by Hermes's stream_events/dispatch 'eat noisy chrome per surface' pattern (PULL the concept).
Argo inherited + hardened Hermes's loop; added autonomous build factory.
Low-friction capture = initiation aid. Argo's 1 adapter is enough for one user; breadth is DROP.
Two independent agents converged here; strong ND fit (kills wrong-guess thrash + blank-page paralysis).
Hermes users hacked this for ADHD; make it native + gentle.
Cheap externalize-context affordance; aligns with Argo's planned @-file mentions.
Prevents 400s on free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama models → reliability.
Explicit accessibility demand in Hermes user-stories (motor needs, blind user). ND-aligned.
Goose deepest, but Argo's consume+serve covers the one-user need.
All three handle it; Hermes most pluggable.
Power-user infra breadth; Argo's kernel already gates.

pulls.note: WORTH ADDING — curated, ND-aligned. The top tier (HIGH) become roadmap items; DEFER items are horizon. Small by design.

items.feature: Task-initiation affordance ('start the next tiny step')
items.why: The activation wall is the central ND exec-function need; NO agent surveyed targets it. Argo's true differentiator — not a copy.

items.feature: clarify tool (ask one structured question before acting)
items.why: Kills wrong-guess thrash; ask-don't-assume = predictable + low-vigilance. Tiny surface, high fit.
Two agents converged. Opt-in act-gate that breaks blank-page paralysis; extends Argo's autonomy L1 into an interactive front door.

items.feature: Top-of-mind reminder (pinned text injected every turn)
items.why: Smallest, highest-fit working-memory offload — 'what am I doing / don't forget X' always in context.
Free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama models 400 on unsupported thinking/effort params. Small declarative map fixes a real reliability gap (echoed by Goose issue #9529).

items.feature: Proactive nudge cadence (gentle native check-ins / standup)
items.why: Hermes users hacked this for ADHD on top of cron; make it native + gentle (not nagging — respect F3).
Undo safety net = low-anxiety trust around destructive ops. Argo's kernel blocks but doesn't snapshot/restore.
Cheap externalize-context affordance; less typing/recall. Already adjacent to Argo's planned U2 @-file mentions — merge them.

items.feature: Typed stream-event contract + per-surface chrome suppression
items.why: Clean agent→surface event vocabulary; surfaces that can't render tool chrome 'eat' it. Reinforces TUI-SMOOTH (calm output). Port the design, not the Python.

items.feature: Voice conversational loop (live mic-in / spoken reply)
items.why: DEFER. Explicit accessibility demand (motor needs, blind user w/ NVDA). Argo has speak/transcribe tools; the live loop is the gap.

items.feature: Recovery / degraded readline CLI (behind a flag)
items.why: DEFER. Resilience when Ink can't render (CI/headless/native-dep failure).
DEFER. A small calm dot, not a dashboard — borders on noise; keep minimal if pulled.

drops.note: LOSE TOTALLY / DO NOT BUILD — fails the ND filter, duplicates Argo, or is platform-thinking-before-users.

items.feature: Adversary mode (LLM reviewer gates each tool call)
items.why: WEAKER duplicate of Argo's kernel: LLM-judged + FAIL-OPEN (reviewer error → call allowed) vs Argo's deterministic fail-closed assess(). Importing it would weaken the moat.
Sharing ecosystem / marketplace = platform-thinking before users; adds choice load (F4 violation).

items.feature: Goose Apps / MCP-UI (embedded HTML mini-apps in chat)
items.why: Adds visual surface + cognitive load; plugin-UI platform-thinking. Argo TUI stays calm.

items.feature: Code Mode (LLM writes JS in Deno runtime to chain tools)
items.why: Pure context/perf optimization for 5+ extensions; new runtime dep. Dev-power-tooling, anti-fit.
Requires external service/token; enterprise noise for a solo ND user.
Multi-agent fleet coordination = platform-thinking. Argo's todo + delegate + swarm cover the single-user need.
Quality-via-ensemble dev tooling; adds cost/latency/noise, no EF benefit.
IDE-integration breadth; Argo is a standalone operator, not an IDE plugin.
Power-user infra breadth; Argo's Rust kernel already gates execution.
Breadth that adds cognitive load + config burden; not an EF need.

items.feature: China/regional platform adapters (QQ, Feishu, WeCom) + i18n desktop
items.why: Platform/locale breadth; Argo is single-user, English, macOS-first.
Argo's 7 providers incl OpenRouter already aggregate models. Duplicate.
Argo has Playwright browser tools. Power-tooling, low ND priority.

items.feature: Full hooks plugin framework (lifecycle shell hooks)
items.why: Config surface + plugin discovery; keep ONLY the notify-on-finish idea (folded into proactive nudges), drop the framework.
Observability/enterprise; no end-user ND value.
Research/training tooling, not an end-user feature.

issues.note: GitHub issue triage (live, 2026-06-04). ND-lens. Both trackers are dominated by dev/platform/provider concerns with near-zero ND framing — which validates the opportunity. Worth-adding items are mostly F6 reliability lessons, not big features.

hermes.title: 'Converse mode' — agent won't act until you say go
hermes.note: Community ask. Plan-first gate; top ND fit (F4/F6). → PULL.

hermes.title: 'Can't type well, voice is huge' (accessibility)
hermes.note: Direct accessibility demand (F1/F3); supports voice-mode PULL.

hermes.title: Google Tasks integration
hermes.note: Task capture = F2/F5; pairs with Argo's todo + gmail/calendar.

hermes.title: Setup wizard loops on stale credential pool entry
hermes.note: F6 — Argo's setup must avoid this onboarding failure mode (initiation friction if onboarding breaks).

hermes.title: Temporary Gemini rate-limits surfaced as 'quota exhausted'
hermes.note: F6 — honest, accurate error mapping (Argo manifesto: report only what's true).

hermes.title: 73% of every API call is fixed overhead
hermes.note: Token-overhead profiling; informs whether Argo ever needs tool_search (not at 33 tools).

hermes.title: CJK/Chinese IME input + Chinese desktop UI
hermes.note: i18n breadth; Argo is English/macOS-first single-user.

goose.title: Read context limit from meta.n_ctx for local servers
goose.note: F6 — reliable auto-compaction for local Ollama/llama.cpp; ties to the per-model capability matrix PULL.

goose.title: SKILL.md relative paths don't work — CWD is workspace not skill dir
goose.note: F6 — Argo's skill execution should set CWD to the skill dir; cheap correctness fix.

goose.title: Register MCP extensions dynamically at runtime
goose.note: Argo already has mount_mcp (live runtime mount, kernel-gated).

goose.title: Image paths containing spaces silently not detected
goose.note: Bug-class lesson: handle spaces in paths; not a feature.

goose.title: Azure Bearer auth / Bedrock GPT / Bedrock streaming

localHermesDelta.note: Customizations in ~/.hermes/hermes-agent vs the clean baseline — what Jason+Claude changed. (stream_* are now upstreamed; ai-gateway + browser_providers remain local-only.)

items.what: Typed frozen-dataclass event vocabulary (MessageChunk/Stop, Commentary, ToolCallChunk/Finished, LongToolHint, GatewayNotice) — names WHAT happened, not HOW delivered.
items.why: Clean agent→surface contract; Argo's TUI feed would benefit from typed events vs ad-hoc callbacks. F3 calm output.

items.what: Per-surface router — an adapter can return None to 'eat' tool chrome on surfaces that can't render it; tool lines join one progress queue (kills draft-vs-tool race).
items.why: 'Eat noisy chrome per surface' IS ND output-regulation (F3). Port the design, not the Python.

items.what: An AI-gateway model-provider plugin (local-only; not in upstream).
items.why: Argo's 7 providers + OpenRouter cover this.

items.what: Pluggable browser backends (local-only; not in upstream).
items.why: Argo has Playwright browser tools.

items.what: Telegram send-draft formatting + cronjob tool adjustments.
items.why: Surface polish; revisit only if Argo's telegram/cron output is noisy (F3).

claudeCode.note: Claude Code CLI survey (leaked-claude-code = partial leak/plumbing layer; claude-code-haha = runnability layer). Study-only; reimplement clean.

items.note: → PULL. Real Argo gap: free-typed models 400 on unsupported params.
Decouples Ask-resolution from the kernel gate; ports cleanly. Argo has no hooks layer.

items.finding: Hooks race the UI prompt (first wins) via stream-json control protocol
items.note: Programmatic policy auto-decides without blocking the user — could reduce approval fatigue. (Pull the idea, not the whole hooks framework.)

items.finding: Recovery/degraded readline CLI behind a flag

items.note: Gateway event-delivery robustness — verify vs Argo's gateway first.
Optional native deps should degrade, never break the input loop.
ANTI-FINDING — do not lift the entrypoint to Node/tsx without stubbing.

items.finding: Not in leak: system prompt, compaction, todo UX, grep/glob/multiedit, thinking, prompt-caching
items.note: Partial leak — cannot source these. Honest gap.

docsTakeaways.hermes: 262 documented user stories. Primary personas: Developers/Technical (65), Personal Assistants (44), Business Ops (16), Content Creators (11), Researchers (9). CRUCIALLY, Hermes DOES have an accessibility/ND thread — but as edge cases, not the spine: voice-first interface ('can't type well, voice is huge' — motor accessibility), ADHD morning/evening standup workflows, proactive unprompted reminders (self-initiation support), memory persistence framed as cognitive-load reduction, and an NVDA screen-reader addon built by a blind user. Tagline 'an agent that grows with you' gestures at democratization beyond developers. TAKEAWAY: the ND/exec-function demand is real and latent in Hermes's own userbase — Hermes treats it as edge, Argo's wedge is to make it THE spine. Validates the nudges / voice / memory / clarify PULLs.
docsTakeaways.goose: Guides cover purely technical infrastructure: sessions, context engineering, recipes, tools, projects, CLI, providers, config, MCP servers, logging, file management, task execution. Explicitly developer-centric ('enhanced code editing', 'codebase analysis', 'terminal integration'); assumes CLI/YAML/API fluency. Secondary enterprise/institutional framing (custom distributions, extension allowlist, sandbox). ZERO accessibility/neurodivergence/executive-function/everyday-assistance framing. Overlap with Argo's ND vision: near-zero — correct and expected. Goose is the contrast case that sharpens Argo's positioning.
